agents. Config drift. Optimizer undoes fixes. Panels data-stale. Goal: consolidate to focused pipeline.

🛡 5/5 PREFLIGHT GATE
Must pass 5 consecutive clean cycles before entering full turbo/night run. Any error resets to 0/5 with automatic self-healing.
📊 HOW PREFLIGHT WORKS
Step-by-Step Preflight Flow
1.
CMD WINDOW CLEANUP — Kill ALL previous ASX/Turbo/Audit/Pipeline/Alert CMD windows. Verify zero old windows remain visually.
2.
SLEEP PREVENTION — Disable standby, hibernate, monitor timeout, screensaver, lock screen. SetThreadExecutionState for Windows. caffeinate for Mac.
3.
LOCK CLEANUP — Remove all stale .git/index.lock, asx_sync.lock, pipeline locks, rebase-merge directories.
4.
GIT SYNC — git pull --ff-only, fix conflicts, ensure latest code from both machines.
5.
SYSTEM AUDIT — Full 10-gate audit: imports, JSON, version guard, rebuild engine, manifest, memory, git safety.
6.
RESOURCE CHECK — CPU, RAM, disk, temperature. Block if RAM >95% or disk >95%.
7.
SNAPSHOT — Backup best_params.json + pipeline_state.json for rollback.
8.
5/5 CLEAN CYCLES — Run 5 full cycles. Each must have:
  • Zero agent failures or timeouts
  • Zero corrupt JSON outputs
  • Heartbeat fresh (<5 min old)
  • RAM <95%, Disk <95%
  • Sleep prevention still active
  • No stale git locks
!
ON ANY FAILURE — Counter resets to 0/5. Then:
  1. AUDIT — import check + JSON validation
  2. FIX — auto_fixer agent runs
  3. LOG — error_map.json + lessons
  4. VERIFY — re-check fixes applied
  5. ARCHIVE — remove conflicting old versions
  Then retry from 0/5.
9.
FULL RUN MODE — All 5 clean. Enter continuous turbo/night loop. Dashboard regenerates. Heartbeat monitors active.

Every strategy mapped to known frameworks (Connors, Minervini, Elder, Bulkowski, Chandelier). Compliance scored. Gaps identified. RULE_102: No unmapped strategies.

⌨ ALL QUICK COMMANDS (27)

Type any of these into Claude Code to trigger the action instantly. No setup needed -- they work every session via CLAUDE.md.

27
COMMANDS
8
SYSTEM
4
TRADING
15
INFRA+REMOTE

Meta Commands

etc
also: and everything else, deep think, what else
When added to any request — deep-think ALL other areas this applies to and proactively add them.
  1. DEEP-THINK — identify ALL areas this change applies to beyond what was asked
  2. AUTO-REGISTER — new command → CLAUDE.md + dashboard + memory
  3. AUTO-REGISTER — new task → dashboard + active_manifest
  4. AUTO-REGISTER — new agent → runner + dashboard + manifest
  5. AUTO-REGISTER — new rule → rulebook + dashboard + CLAUDE.md
  6. AUTO-REGISTER — new strategy → strategy_stack + dashboard + deprecation
  7. AUTO-REGISTER — new error pattern → error_map + lessons_learned
  8. REPORT — tell user what additional areas were found and applied

System & Audit

audit
also: full audit
Run ALL 10 audit gates. Reports GREEN/YELLOW/RED scorecard.
  1. Run session_start.py — Gate 0: session state
  2. Run stale_version.py — Gate 3: stale versions, phantom refs
  3. Run deploy_gate.py — Gate 5: imports, JSON, version guard
  4. Run runtime_verify.py — Gate 9: runner, paper trader, hashes
  5. Run rebuild_engine — Gate 10: 22-check rebuild diagnostic
  6. Run audit_runner — Gate 10: pipeline audit
  7. Run version_guard --report — protected file hashes
  8. Run agent_audit_researcher — improvement proposals
  9. Collect results → scorecard GREEN/YELLOW/RED
status
also: system status, check health
Quick health summary in one glance.
  1. Run version_guard --report — check 0 violations
  2. Read paper trader P&L + open positions
  3. Check research runner PID alive
  4. Check dashboard last regen time
  5. Read turbo_alerts.json for critical alerts
check versions
Version guard + manifest summary.
  1. Run version_guard
  2. Show active_manifest.json summary
  3. Flag any param or version drift
check params
Show TV-locked params across ALL 4 config files.
  1. Read best_params.json
  2. Read best_params.backup.json
  3. Read best_params.reverted.json
  4. Read settings_v2.json
  5. Compare 5 locked params across all files
  6. Flag mismatches with expected values
check stale
Scan for stale versions and phantom references.
  1. Scan runners for phantom agent refs (deleted agents still referenced)
  2. Check archive files not on active paths
  3. Find deprecated files still referenced
  4. Check writable archive files (should be read-only)
save
also: sync, checkpoint, lock it in, wrap up, finalize, save session, push it
Full session save pipeline — audit all new additions, propagate laws, verify, test, commit, push.
  1. LAW PROPAGATION — scan session for unpropagated findings/fixes/rules → memory, rulebook, CLAUDE.md, settings, manifest, dashboard
  2. SESSION LOG — update notes/session_log.md with today's work
  3. AUDIT NEW ADDITIONS — verify every new file/agent/command/rule: correct directory, imports clean, wired to runners/loops/tasks, dashboard panel exists, .bat/.sh twin exists
  4. CONFLICT CHECK — scan for old versions that could interfere. Archive if found.
  5. VERSION GUARD — version_guard --report → 0 violations
  6. JSON INTEGRITY — validate all result JSON files
  7. IMPORT CHECK — verify all pipeline imports clean
  8. DASHBOARD REGEN — generate_dashboard → all panels render
  9. DUAL-MACHINE COMPAT — verify Mac + Desktop compatibility
  10. GIT COMMIT — stage specific files (not git add -A), descriptive message
  11. GIT PUSH — push to origin/main
  12. VERIFY PUSH — confirm success, show commit hash
show rules
Show loop_rulebook.json summary.
  1. Read loop_rulebook.json
  2. Count total rules, show last updated
  3. Group by category
show memory
Show MEMORY.md index.
  1. Read .claude/projects/.../memory/MEMORY.md
  2. List all memory files with descriptions

Trading & Research

check paper
also: paper status
Paper trader status.
  1. Read paper_trader_state.json
  2. Show capital, total P&L, open positions
  3. Show win rate and last trade details
start research
also: start night mode
Start mac_research_runner silently.
  1. nohup python3 -m backtests.pipeline.mac_research_runner > /tmp/asx-research.log 2>&1 &
  2. Runs research agents per cycle in background
stop research
also: stop night mode
Kill mac_research_runner.
  1. pkill -f mac_research_runner
check research
Research runner status.
  1. Check PID: pgrep -f mac_research_runner
  2. Read mac_research_cycle.json for last cycle stats
show research
Research improvement proposals.
  1. Read audit_research.json
  2. Show proposals by priority

Infrastructure

run cleanup
also: cleanup mode
7-phase cleanup. Works on BOTH Mac and Desktop.
  1. Phase 1: AUDIT — auto_fixer + rulebook_enforcer + cross-validator + lessons
  2. Phase 2: FIX — fix all broken agents (missing main, imports, entry)
  3. Phase 3: TEST — import-test every agent, verify main() callable
  4. Phase 4: MAP — log fixes to error_never_again.json + update rulebooks
  5. Phase 5: ARCHIVE — find obsolete/duplicate/.tmp files, clean up
  6. Phase 6: VERIFY — smoke test all fixed agents
  7. Phase 7: REPORT — generate cleanup_report.json + regenerate dashboard
goodnight
also: leaving computer now, start night loop
DESKTOP ONLY. Maximum efficiency overnight mode with 5/5 preflight gate.
  1. Kill ALL previous ASX CMD windows (visual clean slate)
  2. Disable sleep/hibernate/monitor/screensaver/lock screen
  3. Kill bloat processes (Chrome, Teams, Slack, Discord, Spotify, Steam)
  4. Clean ALL git locks + stale files
  5. Run 5/5 PREFLIGHT GATE — 5 consecutive clean cycles required
  6. On any failure: audit → fix → log → verify → archive → retry from 0/5
  7. Once 5/5 passes: enter continuous turbo at max speed (1s between cycles)
  8. Every 3 cycles: check resources, kill bloat, verify sleep prevention
  9. Night report to night_mode_report.json
  10. Watchdog auto-restarts on crash
start turbo mode
DESKTOP ONLY. Start turbo pipeline.
  1. Disable sleep/hibernate
  2. Kill ALL old tasks (auto-push, dashboard, watchdogs, startup)
  3. Install auto_pull_desktop.bat (every 5 min, independent of turbo)
  4. Fix all git state (locks, rebase, merge)
  5. Run 5/5 PREFLIGHT GATE then enter continuous turbo
stop turbo mode
Kill pipeline processes.
  1. Kill all Python pipeline processes
  2. Close the turbo loop window
restart turbo
also: fix turbo
DESKTOP ONLY. Emergency restart.
  1. Kill all pipeline processes
  2. Remove .git/index.lock, rebase-merge, rebase-apply, MERGE_HEAD
  3. Remove .git/asx_sync.lock, pipeline locks
  4. git reset --hard origin/main + git pull
  5. Start turbo pipeline fresh
check turbo
Show turbo heartbeat.
  1. Read turbo_heartbeat.json
  2. Show last cycle time, phase, agent count
start audit mode
also: start diagnostics
Start audit V2.
  1. Mac: nohup ./run_diagnostics_mac.sh > /tmp/asx-diagnostics.log 2>&1 &
  2. Windows: audit.bat on (runs V2 orchestrator audit phase)
  3. Runs V2 critical thinking + system audit
stop audit mode
also: stop diagnostics
Kill audit/diagnostics processes.
  1. Mac: pkill -f diagnostics_mac; pkill -f audit_runner
  2. Windows: taskkill /F /FI "WINDOWTITLE eq ASX Audit Mode"
check audit
also: check diagnostics
Show audit reports.
  1. Read audit_heartbeat.json
  2. Read audit_report.json
open dashboard
Open dashboard.html in browser.
  1. Open dashboard.html in default browser
show tasks
also: list tasks
List all scheduled tasks.
  1. List all scheduled tasks
  2. Show name, schedule, enabled status, last run time

Remote Control (Mac → Desktop)

restart turbo from mac
also: remote restart turbo
MAC ONLY. Send restart command via git.
  1. python3 -m backtests.pipeline.agent_remote_control restart_turbo
  2. Desktop executes on next auto-pull (~5 min)
remote status
Request desktop status.
  1. python3 -m backtests.pipeline.agent_remote_control status
remote git cleanup
Fix desktop git state.
  1. python3 -m backtests.pipeline.agent_remote_control git_cleanup
desktop control
also: control desktop
Interactive desktop control menu.
  1. Run ./desktop_control.sh
  2. Shows status + menu for manual intervention

Control desktop from Mac via git remote commands or SSH/WinRM.

CommandWhat It Does
restart turbo from macSend restart command to desktop via git (~5 min delay)
remote statusRequest desktop status via git remote control
remote git cleanupFix desktop git: abort rebase, clean locks, pull
desktop controlInteractive desktop control menu
show tasksList all scheduled tasks + status
show researchShow research improvement proposals

System DNA Booklet

A complete guide to how this trading system works, what all the parts do, and how to improve it.

Chapter 1: The Big Picture

This system is an autonomous trading research machine. It runs 24/7 across two computers (your Mac and Desktop), continuously researching, testing, and improving ASX stock trading strategies.

The goal: $500/week from multiple uncorrelated strategies trading ASX stocks.

How it works (simplified):

  1. Research Loop runs every ~90 seconds, testing new ideas with real market data
  2. Paper Trader simulates real trades with real prices (no real money yet)
  3. Compound Optimizer continuously tweaks parameters to improve performance
  4. Critical Thinking validates everything -- catches overfitting, fake results
  5. Memory System remembers what worked and what didn't across sessions
  6. When a strategy is proven profitable in paper trading, you validate it on TradingView, then trade it live

Current state: . Paper trading active. Regime-aware output auto-injected into every agent.

Chapter 2: The Body Parts (System Components)

The Brain: CLAUDE.md

This is the system's DNA -- the master instruction file that Claude reads every session. It contains all rules, known bugs, validated research findings, and how everything connects. To improve: Add rules, findings, or constraints directly to CLAUDE.md.

The Memory: Memory Engine V2

Three types of memory that persist across sessions:

  • Episodic (110+ events) -- What happened: trade results, errors, discoveries
  • Semantic (92+ facts) -- What we know: "gold above EMA50 = PF 1.44 for miners"
  • Procedural (13+ strategies) -- How to do things: strategy parameters, entry/exit rules

To improve: Tell Claude "remember that..." or fix incorrect memories with "forget that..."

The Rulebook: 111+ Rules (V2)

Auto-enforced rules that govern everything. Each rule has a priority score and gets enforced every cycle. Rules auto-discover from errors -- if something breaks 5 times, a new rule is created to prevent it.

To improve: Tell Claude "add a rule that..." or check loop_rulebook.json

The Eyes: 193 Research Agents

Specialized workers that each do one thing well:

  • Market agents -- regime detection (BULL/BEAR/SIDEWAYS), sector rotation, correlation tracking
  • Strategy agents -- backtest new ideas, optimize parameters, cross-validate
  • Safety agents -- preflight checks, error analysis, lessons learned, critical thinking
  • Trading agents -- paper trader, portfolio constructor, risk monitor
  • Meta agents -- system architect (finds gaps), improvement engine, quality watchdog

To improve: Tell Claude to build a new agent for any research gap you notice

The Heart: Pipeline Runners

Three runners that keep everything beating:

  • Turbo Runner (Desktop only) -- Full pipeline, 7 phases, parallel execution, auto-commits
  • Mac Research Runner (Mac only) -- 18-phase research loop, V2 orchestrator
  • Night Mode -- Overnight deep research when you're sleeping

To improve: Runners auto-heal -- if an agent crashes, it gets quarantined and retried. Tell Claude if you want different cycle timing or priorities.

The Optimizer: Compound Engine V2

Genetic algorithm + simulated annealing that evolves strategy parameters. It mutates, crossbreeds, and selects the fittest parameter sets. Escapes local optima by random restarts and temperature cooling.

To improve: If stalled (stagnation > 5 cycles), tell Claude to reset it with fresh seed params.

The Skeptic: Critical Thinking V2

12 automated checks that challenge every result:

  • PF > 3.0 in Python? Flag as suspicious (Python overestimates 30-50%)
  • WR > 65%? Requires TV validation proof
  • Parameters drifting? Detect flip-flopping between cycles
  • Edge decaying? Monitor PF trend over time

To improve: Add skepticism rules -- "always check X when Y happens"

The Hands: Paper Trader V2

Simulates real trades with real Yahoo prices. Uses the SAME entry/exit rules as the Pine Script strategies. Tracks P&L, win rate, per-strategy performance. Goal: 50+ trades profitably before going live.

To improve: Monitor paper trades daily. If a strategy consistently loses, tell Claude to disable it.

The Strategies: Pine Scripts

16 Pine Script strategies in TradingView format:

  • v9 Optimized -- Main strategy (MR + RVOL momentum), TV-validated
  • Gap Fill, Weekly Reversal, Monthly Momentum -- Viable candidates awaiting TV validation
  • 8 more in various stages of research

To improve: TV-validate strategies on the desktop. Load the Pine script in TradingView, apply to stocks, record real results.

The Nervous System: Git Sync

Both machines sync via git every 5-20 minutes. Desktop pushes, Mac pulls. This is how research results flow between machines.

To improve: If sync breaks (merge conflicts), tell Claude "fix git" or "remote git cleanup"

Chapter 3: How to Improve the System

Things You Can Say to Claude

"run cleanup"Audit + fix + test all agents
"start turbo mode"Start full pipeline on desktop
"check turbo"See if desktop pipeline is running
"open dashboard"Open this dashboard
"show research"Show latest research findings
"remember that X"Save info to persistent memory
"add a rule that X"Add rule to the rulebook
"what's the bottleneck?"Identify what's slowing progress
"build an agent for X"Create new research agent
"TV validate strategy X"Test a Pine script on TradingView

Priority Improvements (Ranked)

  1. TV-validate more strategies -- We have 14 Pine scripts but only 1 is TV-proven. Open TradingView on desktop, load each .pine file, test on 10+ stocks, record results.
  2. Get paper trader to 50+ trades -- The paper trader now uses real data. Let it run for 2 weeks, monitor daily.
  3. Fix losing strategies -- If paper trader shows a strategy losing, disable it or tighten entry rules.
  4. Expand stock universe -- Currently 68 ASX + 30 US stocks. More stocks = more trading opportunities.
  5. Go live with proven strategy -- Once paper trading shows PF > 1.3 on 50+ trades, start with $5k real money on IB.

Key Files You Should Know

CLAUDE.mdMaster brain -- all rules and knowledge
dashboard.htmlThis dashboard -- auto-refreshes every 30s
notes/session_log.mdLog of every Claude session
strategies/*.pineAll Pine Script strategies for TradingView
backtests/pipeline/results/All research results (JSON)
loop_rulebook.json102 auto-enforced rules
Chapter 4: The Path to Real Money

Stage 1: Research (WE ARE HERE)

, regime-aware outputs. Research is strong.

Stage 2: Paper Trading (JUST STARTED)

6 trades so far ($20,054 capital). Need 50+ profitable trades to prove the edge is real.

Stage 3: TV Validation (1 of 14 done)

v9 validated. 13 more strategies need TradingView confirmation.

Stage 4: Live Trading (NOT YET)

Start with $5k on Interactive Brokers once paper trading proves profitable.

What's Blocking Us

  • #1 Bottleneck: Paper trader only has 5 trades -- need 50+ to prove edge
  • #2 Bottleneck: Only 1 of 14 strategies is TV-validated -- need manual TradingView testing on desktop
  • #3 Bottleneck: Compound optimizer was stalled (fixed today -- reset with fresh seed)

Timeline Estimate

  • This week: Paper trader accumulates 20+ trades, validate 2-3 more Pine scripts on TV
  • Next week: Paper trader at 40+ trades, identify which strategies are consistently profitable
  • Week 3: 50+ paper trades with PF > 1.3 = confidence to go live with small size
  • Week 4: First live trade on IB with $5k allocation
Chapter 5: The Self-Improving DNA

This system is not static. It has DNA — a set of self-improving mechanisms that make it smarter every cycle.

The 5 DNA Layers

  1. CLAUDE.md — Master instructions. Every bug fix, every finding, every rule gets written here. This is the genome that defines how every session behaves.
  2. Memory Engine — Episodic + Semantic + Procedural memory across sessions. Remembers what worked, what failed, user preferences, project context.
  3. Loop Rulebook — 111+ auto-enforced rules. New rules auto-discover from errors (5 failures = new rule). Rules have scores, decay, and enforcement tracking.
  4. Scheduled Tasks — 17 durable tasks that auto-fire every session: trading, system health, research, V3 rebuild. They maintain the system even without user interaction.
  5. Agent Ecosystem — agents across multiple phases. Every agent now auto-injects regime context into its output (regime, benchmark comparison, assumption challenges).

Self-Improvement Mechanisms

Error-Never-AgainEvery error logged to error_map.json. 5+ repeats = auto-rule + auto-fix.
Lessons Learnedagent_lessons_learned scans every cycle, creates lessons from patterns.
Law PropagationEvery finding auto-propagates to ALL systems (memory + rulebook + CLAUDE.md + dashboard).
DNA UpgraderGrades every agent F-A, proposes tier upgrades for regime awareness and analytical depth.
Critical Thinking V212 skepticism checks: PF sanity, param drift, bias detection, edge decay monitoring.
Wiring EnforcerDetects NEW files and auto-links to runners, dashboard, manifest. Nothing gets orphaned.
Regime InjectionEvery agent output auto-enriched with regime context, benchmark comparison, assumptions to challenge.

The DNA Cycle

Error occurs → error_map.json
Pattern detected → lessons_learned.json
Rule created → loop_rulebook.json
Law propagated → CLAUDE.md + memory + dashboard
Agent upgraded → regime-aware output
System evolves → fewer errors next cycle
Result: The system gets smarter every 90 seconds.

8 Quant Systems (Built 2026-04-01)

  1. Walk-Forward Validator — Tests if parameters work on unseen future data (not just history)
  2. Regime Router — Routes trades to the right strategy based on current regime
  3. DNA Upgrader — Grades agents, prescribes tier upgrades
  4. Factor Attribution — Decomposes returns by factor (momentum, value, size, quality)
  5. TCA (Transaction Cost Analysis) — Models real slippage, spread, commission impact
  6. Portfolio Optimizer — Optimal allocation across uncorrelated strategies
  7. Signal Decay Monitor — Detects when a signal is losing edge over time
  8. Execution Simulator — Models realistic order execution on ASX
Chapter 5: Glossary
PFProfit Factor -- total wins / total losses. PF > 1.0 = profitable. PF > 1.5 = good.
WRWin Rate -- % of trades that are profitable. WR > 55% = decent for trend following.
ATRAverage True Range -- measures volatility. Used for stop-loss distances.
MRMean Reversion -- buy when stock drops too far, sell when it bounces back.
MomentumBuy stocks going up strongly, ride the trend.
RVOLRelative Volume -- today's volume vs 20-day average. RVOL > 2x = unusual activity.
Det ScoreDeterioration Score (0-8) -- measures how unhealthy a stock is. 6+ = avoid.
EMAExponential Moving Average -- smoothed price trend. EMA50 > EMA200 = uptrend.
RSI(2)2-period Relative Strength Index. RSI(2) < 12 = extremely oversold = MR entry signal.
Williams %RWR(10) < -90 = extremely oversold. Our best MR signal (PF 8.95).
TurboThe continuous pipeline loop running on desktop. Runs all agents per cycle.
TV ValidationTesting a strategy on real TradingView charts. Python overestimates by 30-50%.
BEAR/BULLMarket regime. BEAR = falling market, trade cautiously. BULL = rising, trade aggressively.
QuarantineWhen an agent crashes repeatedly, it's temporarily disabled and retried later.
PreflightSystem health check that runs before every cycle -- verifies everything is safe.
Chapter 6: The God Complex — System Philosophy

"The system doesn't just trade. It thinks about how it thinks. It improves how it improves. It watches itself watching itself."

The 4 Evolution Stages

Stage 1: NOVICE
Produces output. That's it. No regime context, no why, no recommendations. Just data.
Stage 2: APPRENTICE
Adds context. Knows what regime we're in. Compares to benchmarks. Asks "is this normal?"
Stage 3: PROFICIENT
Explains WHY. Decomposes results by factor. Challenges assumptions. Cross-references other agents.
Stage 4: MASTER
Self-aware. Knows its own biases. Proposes its own upgrades. Questions whether it should even exist.

The Philosophy (RULE_97-100)

RULE_97Every agent must track its own evolution stage and self-assess on every output.
RULE_98Output depth minimum: timestamp + data + WHY + recommendations + regime context. No shells.
RULE_99God Complex ideology: the system must continuously question its own assumptions.
RULE_100Shell agents banned. Every agent must produce substantive analysis, not just a timestamp.

The Grand Master Path

Quant → Understands the math. Knows PF, WR, Kelly, Monte Carlo.
Strategist → Understands WHY the math works. Regime-aware. Factor-attributed.
Grand Master → Questions the strategy itself. Is the edge real? What kills it?
Continuous Evolution → The system improves how it improves. Meta-meta learning.

The Forensic Methodology Law

Every strategy verdict MUST include:

  1. Regime-conditional PF — How does it perform in BULL vs BEAR vs SIDEWAYS?
  2. Benchmark comparison — Does it beat buy-and-hold?
  3. WHY analysis — Why did trades win? Why did they lose? Was it expected?
  4. 25 Quant Questions — Minimum C+ score (14/25) before any strategy goes live.

Root cause this solves: All agents and meta-layers failed to ask "WHY did trades lose?" — they only checked "DID they lose?" A gold miner losing money when gold crashed is EXPECTED, not a strategy failure.

Current State

84
F/D Grade Agents
109
Rules Enforced
5
DNA Layers Active

Many agents still at novice level. The upgrade path: add regime context, add WHY analysis, add self-assessment. One by one, every cycle.

The God-Mode Playbook

Complete guide to every system, capability, and lever. See notes/god_mode_playbook.md for full version.

THE GOD-MODE PLAYBOOK

\n

How to Turn Claude Code Into a Top 0.01% Auto-Trading AI

\n
\n

A complete guide to every system, capability, infrastructure component, and lever you can pull to build the most elite autonomous trading machine possible.

\n
\n

---

\n
\n

VISUAL ARCHITECTURE: THE COMPLETE SYSTEM MAP

\n
\n
\n
                        === THE GOD-MODE ARCHITECTURE ===
\n
\n
    YOU (Bailey)
\n
     |
\n
     v
\n
  [Claude Code CLI / Desktop App / Web App]
\n
     |
\n
     +---> BRAIN LAYER (What Claude Knows)
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- CLAUDE.md .............. Master DNA (rules, findings, strategy)
\n
     |     +-- Memory System .......... Persistent knowledge across sessions
\n
     |     |   +-- MEMORY.md .......... Index (200 lines, loaded every session)
\n
     |     |   +-- user_*.md .......... Who you are, preferences
\n
     |     |   +-- feedback_*.md ...... How to behave (corrections + confirmations)
\n
     |     |   +-- project_*.md ....... Ongoing work context
\n
     |     |   +-- reference_*.md ..... Where to find external info
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Loop Rulebook V2 ....... 102 auto-enforced rules
\n
     |     +-- Memory Engine V2 ....... Episodic + Semantic + Procedural (Bayesian)
\n
     |     +-- Settings V2 ............ 86 platform-aware settings
\n
     |
\n
     +---> EXECUTION LAYER (What Claude Does)
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Tools (Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Grep/Glob)
\n
     |     +-- Agents (parallel subprocesses)
\n
     |     |   +-- general-purpose .... Complex multi-step tasks
\n
     |     |   +-- Explore ............ Fast codebase search
\n
     |     |   +-- Plan ............... Architecture planning
\n
     |     |   +-- claude-code-guide .. Self-knowledge queries
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Skills (slash commands)
\n
     |     |   +-- /commit ............ Auto-commit
\n
     |     |   +-- /simplify .......... Code quality review
\n
     |     |   +-- /loop .............. Recurring tasks
\n
     |     |   +-- /schedule .......... Remote scheduled agents
\n
     |     |   +-- Custom skills ...... YOUR custom workflows
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Hooks .................. Auto-behaviors on events
\n
     |     |   +-- PreToolUse ......... Before any tool runs
\n
     |     |   +-- PostToolUse ........ After any tool runs
\n
     |     |   +-- Notification ....... User notifications
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Cron System ............ In-session recurring tasks
\n
     |     +-- Scheduled Tasks ........ Survive restarts (durable)
\n
     |     +-- Remote Triggers ........ Cloud-scheduled agents (claude.ai API)
\n
     |
\n
     +---> RESEARCH LAYER (How Claude Learns)
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Web Search ............. Live internet search (2026 data)
\n
     |     +-- Web Fetch .............. Read any URL, extract content
\n
     |     +-- BigData/RavenPack ...... Real-time financial data
\n
     |     |   +-- Company tearsheets . Financials, earnings, ESG
\n
     |     |   +-- Market tearsheet ... Global markets, commodities, crypto
\n
     |     |   +-- Country tearsheet .. Economic indicators, G7 comparison
\n
     |     |   +-- Events calendar .... Earnings dates, conferences
\n
     |     |   +-- News search ........ Financial document search
\n
     |     |   +-- Company finder ..... Entity resolution (name->data)
\n
     |     |
\n
     |     +-- Windsor.ai ............. Marketing/data connectors
\n
     |     +-- Notion ................. Knowledge base, trade journals
\n
     |     +-- Jentic APIs ............ 1000s of APIs (news, crypto, etc.)
\n
     |     +-- Microsoft Docs ......... Azure/cloud documentation
\n
     |     +-- Chrome Automation ...... Browse web, fill forms, scrape
\n
     |     +-- Computer Use ........... Control desktop apps directly
\n
     |
\n
     +---> TRADING LAYER (What Makes Money)
\n
           |
\n
           +-- 144 Research Agents .... Autonomous analysis workers
\n
           +-- Paper Trader V2 ........ Real Yahoo prices, multi-strategy
\n
           +-- Compound Engine V2 ..... Genetic + annealing optimization
\n
           +-- Critical Thinking V2 ... 12 automated skepticism checks
\n
           +-- V2 Orchestrator ........ Master coordination layer
\n
           +-- 16 Pine Scripts ........ TradingView strategies
\n
           +-- Preflight V2 ........... 16 pre-cycle health checks
\n
           +-- Runner Health .......... Universal monitoring system
\n
           +-- Turbo Runner ........... Desktop parallel pipeline
\n
           +-- Mac Research Runner .... 18-phase research loop
\n
           +-- Night Mode ............. Overnight deep research
\n
\n
\n
---
\n
\n
GROUP 1: THE BRAIN — Memory, Knowledge & Self-Improvement
\n
\n
1.1 CLAUDE.md (The Master DNA)
\n
\n
**What it is:** The instruction file Claude reads EVERY session. Your project's DNA.
\n
**File:** `/Users/baileyexcell/asx-pine-lab/CLAUDE.md`
\n
**Current size:** ~800 lines, loaded automatically.
\n
\n
How to supercharge it:
\n
- Every validated finding goes here IMMEDIATELY
\n
- Structure: Rules first, then context, then reference data
\n
- Use `` tags on critical rules (Claude respects these)
\n
- Can `@import` other files: `@docs/strategy-rules.md`
\n
- Multiple CLAUDE.md files merge (project root + .claude/ + home dir)
\n
\n
Priority order (highest to lowest):
\n
1. `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (personal, all projects)
\n
2. `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (project-level)
\n
3. `./CLAUDE.md` (project root)
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Keep CLAUDE.md as the "constitution" — immutable core rules. Put volatile data (current bottleneck, latest findings) in memory files instead. This way the DNA stays clean while knowledge grows.
\n
\n
1.2 Memory System (Persistent Knowledge)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Files that persist across conversations. Claude reads MEMORY.md every session and can access individual memory files on demand.
\n
**Location:** `~/.claude/projects/-Users-baileyexcell-asx-pine-lab/memory/`
\n
**Current:** 32 memory files, MEMORY.md index
\n
\n
Memory types (ranked by power):
\nTypePurposePower Level\n
|------|---------|-------------|
\n**feedback**How to behave (corrections + confirmations)HIGHEST — directly changes behavior\n**user**Who you are, your expertise, preferencesHIGH — tailors all responses\n**project**Ongoing work, deadlines, decisionsMEDIUM — provides context\n**reference**Where to find external infoLOW — lookup pointers\n
\n
How to supercharge it:
\n
\n
"Remember that PF > 3.0 in Python is always suspicious"
\n
→ Creates feedback memory → Claude checks this EVERY time it sees PF data
\n
\n
"Remember that our best MR signal is Williams %R(10) < -90"
\n
→ Creates project memory → Claude uses this when building strategies
\n
\n
"Forget the old architecture memory, it's outdated"
\n
→ Removes stale knowledge → Prevents acting on wrong info
\n
\n
\n
God-mode tips:
\n
**Feedback memories are the most powerful.** They literally change Claude's behavior next session. Use them for: "always do X", "never do Y", "when Z happens, check W"
\n
**Keep MEMORY.md under 200 lines** — it's truncated after that
\n
**Update stale memories** — a memory saying "bottleneck is X" from 2 weeks ago will mislead
\n
**Memory + CLAUDE.md = compounding intelligence.** Each session adds knowledge that makes the next session smarter
\n
\n
1.3 Loop Rulebook V2 (Self-Enforcing Rules)
\n
\n
**What it is:** 102 scored rules that agents enforce automatically every cycle.
\n
**File:** `backtests/pipeline/loop_rulebook.json`
\n
\n
How rules self-improve:
\n
1. Agent errors create new rules automatically (error_never_again)
\n
2. Trading losses create new trading rules (agent_trading_rule_discoverer)
\n
3. Rule scores decay if unused (prevents rule bloat)
\n
4. Rules can be retired when superseded
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Tell Claude "add a rule that X" → it goes into the rulebook → EVERY agent on BOTH machines enforces it automatically. This is the most scalable way to improve the system — one rule change propagates everywhere.
\n
\n
1.4 Memory Engine V2 (The AI's Brain)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Three-tier memory system with Bayesian confidence updates.
\n
**File:** `backtests/pipeline/memory_engine_v2.py`
\n
\n
The three tiers:
\n
**Episodic** (110+ events): What happened — trade results, errors, discoveries. Decays over time (0.95^days).
\n
**Semantic** (92+ facts): What we know — "gold above EMA50 = PF 1.44". Confidence updated via Bayesian formula.
\n
**Procedural** (13+ strategies): How to trade — strategy parameters, entry/exit rules.
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** The Bayesian updates mean repeated evidence strengthens beliefs. If 5 different agents all confirm "Williams %R is better than RSI2", the confidence approaches 1.0. Contradictory evidence weakens it. This is how the system learns from data, not just from you.
\n
\n
---
\n
\n
GROUP 2: THE EXECUTION ENGINE — Tools, Agents & Automation
\n
\n
2.1 Agents (Parallel AI Workers)
\n
\n
**What they are:** Specialized sub-processes that work in parallel.
\n
\nAgent TypeWhen to UsePower\n
|------------|-------------|-------|
\n**general-purpose**Complex multi-step tasks, code generationBuild anything\n**Explore**Find files, search code, understand codebaseFast research\n**Plan**Design implementation strategiesArchitecture\n**claude-code-guide**Questions about Claude Code itselfSelf-knowledge\n
\n
How to supercharge:
\n
- Launch multiple agents in PARALLEL — they work simultaneously
\n
- Use `isolation: "worktree"` for risky changes (isolated git branch)
\n
- Agents can call ALL tools — they're full Claude instances
\n
- Use `run_in_background: true` for non-blocking work
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Launch 5 agents simultaneously to research 5 different strategies. Each returns findings. You get 5x the research in the same time. This is the single biggest throughput multiplier.
\n
\n
2.2 Hooks (Auto-Behaviors)
\n
\n
**What they are:** Shell commands that execute automatically when Claude does things.
\n
**File:** `.claude/settings.json`
\n
\n
Available hook events:
\nEventWhen It FiresTrading Use\n
|-------|---------------|-------------|
\n`PreToolUse`Before any tool callAuto-validate before writes\n`PostToolUse`After any tool callAuto-commit after edits\n`Notification`When Claude notifies userPush to phone/Slack\n`Stop`Before Claude stops respondingAuto-save state\n
\n
Example: Auto-commit after every file edit
\n
\n
{
\n
  "hooks": {
\n
    "PostToolUse": [
\n
      {
\n
        "matcher": "Write|Edit",
\n
        "command": "git add -A && git commit -m 'auto-save' --no-verify 2>/dev/null || true"
\n
      }
\n
    ]
\n
  }
\n
}
\n
\n
\n
Example: Run preflight before every Bash command
\n
\n
{
\n
  "hooks": {
\n
    "PreToolUse": [
\n
      {
\n
        "matcher": "Bash",
\n
        "command": "python3 -m backtests.pipeline.agent_preflight_v2 --quick 2>/dev/null"
\n
      }
\n
    ]
\n
  }
\n
}
\n
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Hooks make Claude AUTOMATICALLY do things without being asked. This is how you make every action self-validating, self-committing, and self-healing.
\n
\n
2.3 Scheduled Tasks (Survive Restarts)
\n
\n
**What they are:** Tasks stored in `.claude/scheduled_tasks.json` that persist across sessions.
\n
**Created with:** `mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task`
\n
\n
Examples:
\n
\n
Every day at 9am: Run full system health check
\n
Every hour: Pull latest results, check paper trader
\n
Every 15 min: Health check + auto-restart dead processes
\n
One-time: Remind me to TV-validate gap_fill strategy tomorrow at 2pm
\n
\n
\n
Key options:
\n
- `cronExpression`: Recurring (e.g., "0 9 * * *" = daily 9am)
\n
- `fireAt`: One-time (ISO timestamp)
\n
- `durable: true`: Survives Claude restarts
\n
- `notifyOnCompletion`: Get notified when task finishes
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Create durable scheduled tasks for daily research runs, weekly portfolio reviews, and monthly strategy audits. The system works even when you're not talking to it.
\n
\n
2.4 Remote Triggers (Cloud-Scheduled Agents)
\n
\n
**What they are:** Claude sessions that run on claude.ai servers on a schedule.
\n
**Created with:** `RemoteTrigger` tool
\n
\n
This is the MOST POWERFUL automation:
\n
- Runs on Anthropic's servers, not your machine
\n
- Can execute on a cron schedule
\n
- Full Claude capability (tools, web search, etc.)
\n
- Returns results you can check later
\n
\n
Example: Daily market research briefing
\n
\n
Create trigger: "Every morning at 7am, search for latest ASX market news,
\n
check gold price, check ASX200 level, check any earnings announcements
\n
for our watchlist stocks. Write a briefing to Notion."
\n
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Remote triggers + Notion = a research army that works 24/7 without your Mac running. Each trigger is a full Claude session with all tools.
\n
\n
2.5 Skills (Custom Commands)
\n
\n
**What they are:** Reusable prompt templates you create.
\n
**Location:** `.claude/skills/` or created via `/skill-creator`
\n
\n
Current built-in skills:
\n
- `/commit` — smart git commit
\n
- `/simplify` — code quality review
\n
- `/loop` — recurring prompt execution
\n
- `/schedule` — remote agent scheduling
\n
- `/pdf`, `/xlsx`, `/pptx`, `/docx` — document creation
\n
\n
How to create custom trading skills:
\n
\n
/skill-creator
\n
\n
Name: "backtest"
\n
Description: "Run a quick backtest on specified strategy and stocks"
\n
Prompt: "Run backtests.pipeline.agent_paper_trader_v2 with --strategy {strategy}
\n
         on {stocks}. Show PF, WR, trade count. Compare to baseline."
\n
\n
\n
Custom skill ideas for trading:
\nSkillPurpose\n
|-------|---------|
\n`/bt {strategy}`Quick backtest any strategy\n`/tv-check`Compare Python results vs TradingView\n`/market-brief`Morning market briefing\n`/trade-review`Review paper trader performance\n`/strategy-audit`Full audit of one strategy\n`/pine {strategy}`Generate Pine Script from research\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Skills are like macros — one word triggers a complex workflow. Build skills for every repeated task.
\n
\n
2.6 Cron System (In-Session Recurring)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Tasks that repeat within the current Claude session.
\n
**Created with:** `CronCreate` tool
\n
\n
Current use in our project:
\n
- Health check every 15 minutes
\n
- Auto-restart dead research loop
\n
- Monitor paper trader positions
\n
\n
**Limitation:** Dies when Claude session ends. Use Scheduled Tasks for persistence.
\n
\n
---
\n
\n
GROUP 3: THE RESEARCH POWERHOUSE — How Claude Learns From the World
\n
\n
3.1 Web Search (Live Internet)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Real-time web search with 2026 data.
\n
**When to use:** Market news, economic events, sector analysis, competitor strategies
\n
\n
Trading applications:
\n
- Search "ASX gold miners news today" → live market sentiment
\n
- Search "RBA interest rate decision 2026" → macro context
\n
- Search "momentum trading strategies academic papers 2025 2026" → new ideas
\n
- Search "Interactive Brokers API python 2026" → execution tech
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Combine web search with memory storage. Search → find insight → store in Memory V2 → system gets smarter permanently.
\n
\n
3.2 Web Fetch (Read Any URL)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Read and extract content from any webpage.
\n
**When to use:** Deep-dive on specific articles, documentation, data sources
\n
\n
Trading applications:
\n
- Fetch ASX announcements pages
\n
- Read academic papers on quantitative strategies
\n
- Extract data from financial blogs
\n
- Read API documentation for new data sources
\n
\n
3.3 BigData / RavenPack (Financial Intelligence)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Professional-grade financial data via MCP.
\n
Tools available:
\n
\nToolDataTrading Use\n
|------|------|-------------|
\n`find_companies`Entity resolutionFind any company's ID\n`bigdata_company_tearsheet`Full financials, earnings, ESGDeep company analysis\n`bigdata_market_tearsheet`Global markets, commodities, cryptoMarket regime detection\n`bigdata_country_tearsheet`Economic calendar, GDP, CPIMacro context\n`bigdata_events_calendar`Earnings dates, conferencesEvent-driven trading\n`bigdata_search`News, transcripts, filingsSentiment analysis\n
\n
Example: Real-time market regime detection
\n
\n
1. bigdata_market_tearsheet → get gold, ASX200, VIX, USD/AUD levels
\n
2. bigdata_country_tearsheet("AU") → upcoming RBA decision, CPI
\n
3. bigdata_events_calendar → BHP/RIO/FMG earnings dates
\n
4. bigdata_search("ASX mining sector outlook") → sentiment
\n
5. Store findings in Memory V2 → update regime_ml.json
\n
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** This is REAL financial data — the same quality that hedge funds use. Wire it into the research loop to replace Yahoo Finance for market context. Keep Yahoo for price data (free, reliable), use RavenPack for intelligence.
\n
\n
3.4 Notion Integration (Knowledge Base)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Full Notion workspace access — create, read, update pages and databases.
\n
**Tools:** `notion-search`, `notion-fetch`, `notion-create-pages`, `notion-create-database`
\n
\n
Trading applications:
\n
**Trade journal database** — every paper trade logged with entry reason, exit, P&L
\n
**Strategy research wiki** — organized findings per strategy
\n
**Daily briefing page** — auto-generated each morning
\n
**Watchlist database** — stocks with real-time notes
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Create a Notion database for your trade journal. Have the paper trader V2 auto-log every trade to Notion. This gives you a searchable, visual history of everything the system does.
\n
\n
3.5 Chrome Automation (Browser Control)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Full browser control — navigate, click, type, screenshot, read pages.
\n
**Tools:** `navigate`, `find`, `computer`, `read_page`, `screenshot`
\n
\n
Trading applications:
\n
**Automate TradingView validation** — load Pine script, apply to chart, screenshot results
\n
**Scrape ASX data** — announcement pages, broker reports
\n
**Monitor live positions** — check IB portfolio in browser
\n
**Create GIF recordings** — document trading setups
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** This is how you automate TV validation from Mac. Navigate to TradingView → load strategy → apply to BHP → screenshot → read results → store in tv_validation.json. No manual work.
\n
\n
3.6 Computer Use (Desktop App Control)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Direct control of ANY desktop application — click, type, read screen.
\n
**Tools:** `screenshot`, `left_click`, `type`, `key`, `scroll`
\n
\n
Trading applications:
\n
- Control TradingView desktop app directly
\n
- Control Interactive Brokers TWS for paper trading
\n
- Automate any desktop workflow
\n
\n
**God-mode tip:** Combined with `teach_mode`, you can have Claude walk you through complex TradingView workflows step-by-step with visual tooltips.
\n
\n
3.7 Jentic API Hub (1000s of APIs)
\n
\n
**What it is:** Access to hundreds of REST APIs without managing auth.
\n
**Tools:** `search_apis`, `load_execution_info`, `execute`
\n
\n
Trading applications:
\n
**NewsAPI** — real-time news for watchlist stocks
\n
**Finnhub** — stock quotes, earnings, filings
\n
**CoinCap** — crypto prices (if expanding to crypto)
\n
**Any API** — search for what you need, execute directly
\n
\n
Example: Live stock quote
\n
\n
search_apis("stock quote price") → find Finnhub quote API
\n
execute(op_id, {"symbol": "BHP.AX"}) → real-time price
\n
\n
\n
---
\n
\n
GROUP 4: TIER LIST — From Zero to God-Mode
\n
\n
Tier S (Implement IMMEDIATELY — Biggest Impact)
\n
\nSystemActionWhy\n
|--------|--------|-----|
\n**Scheduled Tasks (durable)**Create daily 7am market briefingResearch runs while you sleep\n**Remote Triggers**Schedule overnight research on claude.ai servers24/7 intelligence gathering\n**BigData Market Tearsheet**Wire into regime detectionReal hedge-fund-grade data\n**Feedback Memories**Save every lesson learnedSystem never makes same mistake twice\n**Chrome + TradingView**Automate TV validationEliminates #1 bottleneck\n
\n
Tier A (Implement This Week — High Impact)
\n
\nSystemActionWhy\n
|--------|--------|-----|
\n**Custom Skills**Create /bt, /tv-check, /market-briefOne-word complex workflows\n**Notion Trade Journal**Auto-log every paper tradeSearchable history + learning\n**Hooks (PostToolUse)**Auto-commit after editsNever lose work\n**Parallel Agents**5 agents researching simultaneously5x throughput\n**BigData Events Calendar**Wire earnings dates into paper traderEvent-driven edge\n
\n
Tier B (Implement This Month — Medium Impact)
\n
\nSystemActionWhy\n
|--------|--------|-----|
\n**Jentic APIs**Add NewsAPI for sentimentMarket-moving news alerts\n**Computer Use**Control IB TWS directlyAutomate order execution\n**Plan Mode**Use for major architecture decisionsBetter design upfront\n**Worktrees**Isolated testing of risky changesSafe experimentation\n**Skills (/loop)**Recurring status checksContinuous monitoring\n
\n
Tier C (Implement Later — Foundation)
\n
\nSystemActionWhy\n
|--------|--------|-----|
\n**CLAUDE.md imports**Split into focused filesCleaner DNA management\n**Keybindings**Custom shortcuts for trading opsSpeed\n**Launch.json**Dev server configsDashboard serving\n**Todo system**Task tracking for sessionsOrganization\n
\n
---
\n
\n

\n
\n
---
\n
\n
GROUP 6: HOW TO MAKE IT SELF-IMPROVING
\n
\n
The Self-Improvement Loop
\n
\n
\n
1. EXPERIENCE — Paper trader takes trades with real data
\n
     |
\n
2. OBSERVE — Agents analyze what happened (win/loss/why)
\n
     |
\n
3. LEARN — Memory V2 stores lessons (Bayesian confidence)
\n
     |
\n
4. ADAPT — Compound V2 evolves parameters
\n
     |
\n
5. ENFORCE — Rulebook V2 adds new rules from errors
\n
     |
\n
6. VALIDATE — Critical Thinking V2 challenges changes
\n
     |
\n
7. IMPROVE — Changes propagate to ALL systems
\n
     |
\n
     +---> Back to 1 (every 90 seconds)
\n
\n
\n
How to Accelerate Self-Improvement
\n
\n
1. Feed it more data:
\n
- Wire BigData market tearsheet into every cycle
\n
- Add NewsAPI for real-time sentiment
\n
- Expand stock universe (US stocks = more liquid, more trades)
\n
\n
2. Feed it more experience:
\n
- Run paper trader 24/7 (durable scheduled tasks)
\n
- Test on historical data AND live data simultaneously
\n
- Multiple strategies = more trades = faster learning
\n
\n
3. Feed it more skepticism:
\n
- Every result must pass Critical Thinking V2
\n
- Every Python PF > 2.0 gets flagged
\n
- Cross-validate everything against TradingView
\n
\n
4. Feed it more rules:
\n
- Every error becomes a rule (error_never_again)
\n
- Every trading loss becomes a trading rule
\n
- Rules decay if unused (prevents bloat)
\n
\n
5. Feed it more memory:
\n
- Feedback memories change behavior permanently
\n
- Project memories provide context
\n
- Episodic memory decays (0.95^days) — only strong signals persist
\n
\n
The God-Mode Formula
\n
\n
\n
GOD MODE = (Data Sources x Research Agents x Self-Improving Rules)
\n
           + (Real Trading Experience x Memory Persistence)
\n
           + (Parallel Execution x Scheduled Automation)
\n
           / (Time to Learn)
\n
\n
\n
Every lever you pull multiplies the others.
\n
- More data → better research → better rules → better trades → more data
\n
- More memory → fewer mistakes → faster learning → more memory
\n
- More automation → more cycles → more experience → more automation
\n
\n
This is the compounding flywheel. Once it starts spinning, it accelerates.
\n
\n
---
\n
\n
GROUP 7: COMMUNICATION — How Systems Talk to Each Other
\n
\n
Internal Communication
\n
\n
\n
Message Bus V2 (message_bus_v2.json)
\n
  |
\n
  +-- Paper Trader → "entered BHP at $42.50"
\n
  +-- Regime Detector → "regime changed to BEAR"
\n
  +-- Compound V2 → "new best params found"
\n
  +-- Critical Thinking → "WARNING: PF suspicious"
\n
  +-- Preflight → "HALT: disk full"
\n
  |
\n
  All agents can subscribe to any message type
\n
\n
\n
External Communication
\n
\nChannelHowStatus\n
|---------|-----|--------|
\n**Dashboard**Auto-refreshes every 30sACTIVE\n**Git sync**Results push to GitHub every 20 minACTIVE\n**Notion**Can auto-post trade journal entriesAVAILABLE (not wired)\n**Terminal**Runner health prints status every 15 minACTIVE\n**Scheduled tasks**Notifications on completionAVAILABLE\n
\n
How to Wire Up Alerts
\n
\n
Notion alerts (recommended):
\n
\n
When paper trader takes a trade → create Notion page in Trade Journal
\n
When PF drops below 1.0 → create Notion alert page
\n
When new strategy is viable → add to Strategy Pipeline database
\n
\n
\n
Terminal alerts (current):
\n
\n
Runner health prints: "ALIVE | Cycle 42 | 38/40 OK | PF 1.3 | PT: 8 trades $+240"
\n
Every 15 minutes while running
\n
\n
\n
---
\n
\n

\n
\n
Core Config

\n
~/.claude/projects/.../memory/*.md           → Individual memories
\n
\n
\n

\n
\n

\n
backtests/pipeline/loop_rulebook.json        → 102 rules
\n
backtests/pipeline/mac_research_runner.py    → Mac loop
\n
backtests/pipeline/turbo_runner.py           → Desktop loop
\n
backtests/pipeline/agent_paper_trader_v2.py  → Paper trader (real data)
\n
backtests/pipeline/agent_preflight_v2.py     → Pre-cycle health checks
\n
backtests/pipeline/runner_health.py          → Universal monitoring
\n
backtests/pipeline/memory_engine_v2.py       → AI memory system
\n
backtests/pipeline/rulebook_v2.py            → Self-enforcing rules
\n

\n
backtests/pipeline/critical_thinking_v2.py   → Skepticism engine
\n

\n
backtests/pipeline/generate_dashboard.py     → Dashboard generator
\n
\n
\n
Strategies
\n
\n
strategies/asx_optimized_v9.pine             → Main strategy (TV-validated)
\n
strategies/asx_gap_fill_v1.pine              → Gap fill (needs TV validation)
\n
strategies/asx_weekly_reversal_v1.pine       → Weekly reversal (needs TV)
\n
strategies/asx_keltner_squeeze_v1.pine       → Keltner squeeze
\n
indicators/asx_hybrid_scanner_v8.pine        → Primary scanner
\n
watchlists/asx200_validated_winners.txt      → 68 validated stocks
\n
\n
\n
Monitoring
\n
\n
dashboard.html                               → Main dashboard
\n
notes/session_log.md                         → Session history
\n
notes/god_mode_playbook.md                   → THIS FILE
\n
/tmp/asx-research-loop.log                   → Mac loop output
\n
turbo_crash_log.txt                          → Desktop crash history
\n

\n
\n
This playbook is a living document. Every session should make it more accurate.
\n
Last updated: 2026-03-30
\n

⚠ Decision Auditor

Watches your session work and flags when you're heading in the wrong direction. Runs every research cycle.

10
FOCUS SCORE / 100
CRITICAL_DRIFT
Almost all work is infrastructure. NO path to $500/week visible in recent activity.
TOP RECOMMENDATION
The bottleneck is NOT infra. Paper trader is running. You need TV validation of Pine scripts, not more Python plumbing.
Paper Trades: 7
Paper P&L: $0
Infra Commit %: 78%
TV Validated: YES
Last audit: 2026-04-04T09:10:35

⚠ Active Warnings

HIGH — INFRA_OVER_TRADING
Building more infrastructure instead of generating trades.
→ The bottleneck is NOT infra. Paper trader is running. You need TV validation of Pine scripts, not more Python plumbing.
Detected: runner, pipeline, health check in recent work
CRITICAL — PF_WITHOUT_TV
Trusting Python PF numbers without TradingView validation.
→ Python overestimates by 30-88%. v8 was PF 8.34 Python -> PF 0.58 TradingView. ALWAYS validate on TV before celebrating.
Detected: pf 2., pf 3., pf 4. in recent work
MEDIUM — DASHBOARD_OVER_TRADING
Spending session time on dashboard aesthetics vs trading logic.
→ Dashboard is a reporting tool, not the product. If you haven't validated a Pine script this week, that's the priority.
Detected: dashboard, panel, booklet in recent work
MEDIUM — AGENT_PROLIFERATION
Adding more agents when existing agents aren't producing actionable results.
→ You have 144+ agents. More agents ≠ more money. Ask: which existing agent output have you acted on this week?
Detected: new agent, add agent in recent work
HIGH — BOTTLENECK_IGNORED
Generating documentation when the stated bottleneck is TV validation.
→ Every minute on docs is a minute not validating Pine scripts. Current bottleneck: TV test 3 scripts on desktop.
Detected: booklet in recent work

[x] Positives

✓ TradingView validation happening -- gold standard being maintained.
How This Works
The Decision Auditor runs every research cycle (~90s). It reads your git commit history, session log, and paper trader state to evaluate whether your work is moving toward $500/week trading income. It checks 10 known anti-patterns (infra drift, PF without TV validation, agent proliferation, etc.) and scores your session from 0-100. Score below 60 = you're heading the wrong way.

Hooks, Keybindings & MCP Booklet

Everything you can configure to make Claude Code faster, smarter, and more autonomous.

What Are Hooks?

Hooks are shell commands that Claude Code runs automatically when specific events happen. Think of them as triggers: "after every file I edit, run this command" or "before every bash command, check this condition." They live in .claude/settings.json under the "hooks" key.

Hooks fire on: PreToolUse (before a tool runs) | PostToolUse (after a tool runs) | Notification | Stop

Recommended Hooks for This Project

Hook Trigger Why
git add -A && git commit -m "auto-save" PostToolUse: Write/Edit Every file change auto-committed so auto_pull on Mac never loses your work
python3 -m backtests.pipeline.safety_hooks PreToolUse: Bash Safety check before any bash command runs
python3 backtests/pipeline/generate_dashboard.py PostToolUse: Write Regenerate dashboard every time any file changes
afplay /System/Library/Sounds/Glass.aiff Stop Audible chime when Claude finishes a task (Mac only)
Add hooks to .claude/settings.json:
"hooks": {
  "PostToolUse": [
    {
      "matcher": "Write|Edit",
      "hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": "cd /Users/baileyexcell/asx-pine-lab && git add -A && git commit -m 'auto-save [hook]' 2>/dev/null || true"}]
    }
  ],
  "Stop": [
    {"hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": "afplay /System/Library/Sounds/Glass.aiff 2>/dev/null || true"}]}
  ]
}
Note: Auto-save hook disabled on Desktop -- turbo handles its own commits. Mac only.

What Are Keybindings?

Keybindings are keyboard shortcuts inside the Claude Code CLI that trigger actions instantly. They're configured in VS Code (keybindings.json) or in terminal profile. Most useful for triggering Claude commands, running pipeline checks, or switching between views.

Built-in Claude Code Shortcuts

Key Action
Ctrl+CStop Claude mid-response
Up ArrowNavigate command history
EscapeClear input / cancel
/helpList all slash commands
/fastToggle fast mode (same Opus model, faster output)
/clearClear context window (start fresh without closing)
/modelSwitch model mid-session

What is MCP?

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard that lets Claude connect to external tools, APIs, and services. Think of it like plugins for Claude -- each MCP server adds a new superpower. They appear as tools Claude can call (like mcp__bigdata__find_companies) and are configured in .claude/settings.json.

CRITICAL LIMITATION: MCP tools ONLY work when Claude Code is running (i.e., when you're in a live session). Your Python research loop CANNOT call MCP tools directly. Solution: Claude writes MCP findings to JSON files -> Python loop reads them.

Your Active MCP Servers

Server What It Does Best Use for Trading
BigData / RavenPack Professional financial data -- company tearsheets, market events, economic calendar Pre-trade check: any earnings/news for stocks you're about to enter
Jentic / API Search Finds and executes APIs -- discover new data sources Find ASX-specific data APIs the loop can use
Notion Read/write your Notion workspace Trade journal synced to Notion automatically
Claude in Chrome Control Chrome -- navigate, read pages, fill forms Scrape ASX announcements, read TradingView
Computer Use Full desktop control -- click, type, screenshot Automate TradingView validation (click, screenshot results)
Scheduled Tasks Create persistent tasks that run on a schedule Daily market briefing, hourly paper trader check
Preview Serve and interact with web apps Test dashboard changes without opening a browser
Microsoft Docs Search Microsoft documentation Low value for trading

Scheduled Tasks vs Turbo -- What's the Difference?

Turbo Pipeline (Desktop) Scheduled Tasks (Claude)
What runs Python agents -- backtests, optimizers, signal discovery Claude intelligence -- MCP tools, web search, BigData API
Needs computer on? YES -- runs on Desktop (BAZIL) YES -- local tasks need computer on
Can use MCP? NO YES
Frequency Every ~90 seconds continuously Hourly / daily (configurable)
Purpose Heavy computation, strategy research, backtesting Market intelligence, news, economic events, briefings
Output results/*.json -- read by dashboard JSON files -> Python loop reads -> acts on

They're complementary layers, not alternatives. Turbo = the engine, Scheduled Tasks = the intelligence layer on top.

What Are Custom Skills (Slash Commands)?

Custom skills are shortcuts you type in Claude Code (e.g., /bt) that expand into a full, detailed instruction. They're stored in .claude/skills/ as .md files. Instead of typing the same long prompt every time, you type one word.

Recommended Skills for This Project

Command What It Does
/btRun full backtest audit -- check results, TV validate, summary
/tv-checkReview latest TradingView validation results and compare to Python
/market-briefToday's macro: gold, ASX200, regime, what to trade today
/trade-reviewReview paper trader recent trades, WR, P&L, what to fix
/session-startRun startup checklist: pull, auto_learnings, mission, night report
/focus-checkRun Decision Auditor instantly -- am I working on the right thing?
Skills files go in: /Users/baileyexcell/asx-pine-lab/.claude/skills/bt.md etc.
Auto-Save Note

Mac: Auto-save hook can be enabled -- every Write/Edit auto-commits. This is SAFE on Mac because Mac never pushes during turbo.
Desktop (BAZIL): Auto-save is NOT recommended via hook -- turbo handles its own commits every 20 min. Double-committing creates noise in git log.
Current status: Auto-save hook is NOW CONFIGURED (PostToolUse Write/Edit -> git commit). Desktop: turbo handles commits, hook not needed.

Value: THE GOAL. This is what everything is building toward. Revenue.

All Capabilities

MCP Server Capabilities

BigData/RavenPack
ACTIVE   HIGH
Professional financial data: company tearsheets, event calendars, market sentiment
💹 Pre-trade catalyst check. Call bigdata_company_tearsheet before entering any position.
→ Write Claude scheduled task that calls BigData API daily → saves to results/daily_catalysts.json → Python loop reads
Value: Prevents entering stocks on earnings days, major events. Avoid nasty surprises.
Jentic/API Search
ACTIVE   MEDIUM
Discovers and executes any REST API. Can find ASX-specific data sources.
💹 Find ASX announcements API, CMC Markets data, CommSec sector data
→ Use search_apis to find ASX data endpoints → test → wire into Python loop as new data sources
Value: Could unlock real-time ASX price data, corporate announcements, short interest data
Notion MCP
ACTIVE   MEDIUM
Read/write Notion workspace. Create pages, databases, update properties.
💹 Automated trade journal. Every paper trade → Notion page with entry/exit/thesis.
→ Wire paper trader V2 to write trade summaries → Claude scheduled task pushes to Notion
Value: Persistent, searchable trade history. Review patterns across months.
Computer Use MCP
ACTIVE   CRITICAL
Full desktop control: click, type, screenshot, scroll
💹 Automate TradingView validation. Open chart, run backtest, screenshot results, parse PF/WR.
→ Script: open Chrome → navigate to TV → paste Pine script → run backtest → screenshot → parse results → save to tv_validation.json
Value: ELIMINATES the #1 bottleneck (manual TV validation). Could run 10x faster than manual.
Claude in Chrome
ACTIVE   HIGH
Navigate Chrome, read page content, fill forms
💹 Scrape ASX announcements from asx.com.au. Read news. Monitor stock pages.
→ Scheduled task: navigate asx.com.au/announcements → extract company announcements → save to results/asx_announcements.json
Value: Real-time ASX announcement awareness. News-driven entries.
Scheduled Tasks
ACTIVE   HIGH
Create persistent tasks that run on schedule (local, needs Mac on)
💹 Daily 9am market briefing. Hourly paper trader health check. Pre-market regime check.
→ mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task with cron expression
Value: Intelligence even when not in active session. Market context always fresh.

Hooks System

PostToolUse Write/Edit Hook
CONFIGURED   HIGH
Auto-commits every file change
💹 Never lose code. Mac never gets stale.
→ Already in .claude/settings.json — auto-commits on every Write/Edit
Value: Eliminates risk of auto_pull wiping uncommitted work
Stop Hook (Sound Alert)
CONFIGURED   LOW
Audible chime when Claude finishes
💹 Know when a research cycle completes without watching screen
→ Already in .claude/settings.json — plays Glass.aiff on Stop
Value: Better UX — step away and get notified
PreToolUse Safety Hook
RECOMMENDED   MEDIUM
Run safety check before any Bash command
💹 Prevent accidentally running destructive commands
→ Add PreToolUse hook with safety_hooks.py validation
Value: Extra safety layer. Catch dangerous git operations before they run.

Infrastructure Gaps & Additions

Remote Triggers (Cloud Claude)
NOT_CONFIGURED   HIGH
Schedule Claude agents that run on Anthropic servers — no computer needed
💹 Overnight market analysis that runs regardless of whether Mac/Desktop is on
→ RemoteTrigger tool → create cloud-scheduled agent with cron schedule

Custom Skills (/commands)
NOT_CONFIGURED   MEDIUM
Slash commands that expand to detailed prompts
💹 /focus-check, /bt, /market-brief, /trade-review, /session-start
→ Create .claude/skills/*.md files for each command

PENDING_MERGE   CRITICAL

→ Merge mac-v2-systems branch on Desktop. Wire v2_orchestrator into turbo_runner.

Pine Script Auto-Validator
CONCEPT   CRITICAL
Use Computer Use MCP to automate TradingView validation

→ agent_tv_auto_validator.py → launches Chrome → Computer Use drives TradingView → parses results

Notification System
CONCEPT   MEDIUM
Push notifications to phone when important events happen
💹 Alert when paper trader WR drops below 40%, new signal fire, pipeline crash
→ Use Jentic to find push notification API → webhook → mobile alerts
Value: True passive monitoring. Know about issues before the next session.
IBKR API Integration

Connect to Interactive Brokers API for live order execution

→ ib_insync library → agent_live_trader.py → starts with $0 (paper) → graduate to real
Value: THE GOAL. This is what everything is building toward. Revenue.

How These Work
These run as Claude agents on a schedule. Each writes JSON to results/ -> Python loop reads it -> dashboard shows it.
They have access to: BigData API, web search, Chrome browser, scheduled task management.
They DON'T need you present -- they run automatically while Mac is on.
View/manage them: Claude app sidebar -> Scheduled section.

📄 File Health Audit

Validates all 350+ JSON result files every 3rd research cycle. Checks structure, settings defaults, cross-platform consistency.

HEALTH SCORE / 100

Settings Audit
settings_v2.json: OK (machine: mac)
loop_rulebook.json: ISSUES (0 rules)
Last: 2026-04-01T22:18:10

Files with Issues

⚠ overnight_premium.json: Contains bare null or Infinity -- invalid JSON, will break consumers
⚠ simulation_results.json: Contains bare null or Infinity -- invalid JSON, will break consumers
Runs every 3rd research cycle (~4.5 min). Also runs as scheduled task every 2 hours via Claude intelligence layer. Auto-fixes: adds missing timestamps, normalises schema. Flags but never corrupts.

📚 System Guide & Configuration Reference

How every system works, how to use it, and what settings control it. Updated 2026-03-30.

1. HOOKS (What runs automatically)

HookTriggerWhat It DoesFile
Stop HookClaude session endsCommits all changes + plays sound.claude/settings.json
Pre-commitBefore every git commitValidates JSON + Python syntax (blocks bad commits).git/hooks/pre-commit

REMOVED 2026-03-30: PostToolUse auto-commit hook. Was running git add -A on every edit, causing 31+ junk commits and rebase cascades.

REMOVED 2026-03-30: Dashboard regen in pre-commit. Pipeline handles dashboard generation now (turbo Phase 7).

Rule: No hook may trigger another hook. No hook may run git add -A. Pre-commit = validation only.

2. MEMORIES (.claude/projects/.../memory/)

Persistent notes Claude reads every session. Types: user (who you are), feedback (how to work), project (what's happening), reference (where things are).

  • Index: MEMORY.md -- one-line pointers to memory files
  • Files: Individual .md files with YAML frontmatter (name, description, type)
  • Auto-created: Claude saves memories during sessions when it learns something useful
  • Cross-machine: Stored in ~/.claude/projects/ -- NOT synced via git (machine-local)

To remember something: Tell Claude "remember that..." and it saves immediately.

To forget: Tell Claude "forget the memory about..." and it removes it.

3. SAVING & GIT (How changes persist)

WhenWhat HappensWho Does It
Session endgit add -A && commit with --no-verifyStop hook (Mac)
Manual commitClaude runs git commit with specific filesClaude (when asked)
Every 5 minDesktop auto-pull (git sync)auto_pull_desktop.bat
Every 5 minMac auto-pull (read-only sync)auto_pull.sh
Every turbo cycleResults + dashboard committedturbo_runner.py

NEVER: git add -A in automation. git reset --hard anywhere. git checkout -- . (use scoped paths).

ALWAYS: Stage specific files. Use --no-verify for auto-commits. Use atomic writes (tmp+rename).

4. DUAL SESSIONS (Mac + Desktop simultaneously)

Both machines can run Claude interactive sessions at the same time. Session lock is for awareness, not blocking.

MachineRolesGit Access
MacInteractive editing + research loopPull every 5min. Push on commit.
Desktop (BAZIL)Turbo pipeline + interactive editingPush results every 20min + turbo syncs

Conflict prevention: Scoped commits (each machine stages its own files, not git add -A). Dashboard regen only in pipeline.

Session lock: results/session_lock.json shows all active sessions. Warns but doesn't block.

If conflicts happen: git pull --ff-only first, then --rebase, then manual resolve. Never --hard.

5. CONFIGS & SETTINGS

FileWhat It ControlsSynced?
.claude/settings.jsonHooks, permissions, plugins, modelYes (git)
loop_rulebook.json130+ rules enforced every pipeline cycleYes (git)
loop_settings.jsonPipeline parameters (intervals, thresholds)Yes (git)
system_settings_v2.py86 platform-aware settings (Mac vs Desktop)Yes (git)
.git/hooks/pre-commitGit pre-commit validationNO (.git not synced)
~/.claude/projects/.../memory/Claude memoriesNO (machine-local)

To change a setting: Edit the file. If it's in git, it auto-applies to both machines on next pull.

Pre-commit hook: Must be manually created on each machine (it's in .git/ which isn't tracked).

6. RULES (loop_rulebook.json)

130+ mandatory rules enforced automatically every pipeline cycle by agent_rulebook_enforcer.

  • Added automatically: agent_rule_discoverer creates rules from error patterns
  • Added manually: Claude adds rules during sessions when bugs/preferences are found
  • Enforced: Every turbo + research cycle checks all rules. Violations logged.
  • Priority: CRITICAL (must fix immediately) > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW

To add a rule: Tell Claude "add a rule that..." -- it writes to loop_rulebook.json immediately.

7. FILES & ARCHITECTURE

PathPurpose
strategies/*.pineActive Pine Script strategies (current best versions only)
indicators/*.pineActive Pine Script scanners/indicators
backtests/pipeline/agent_*.py~144 pipeline agents
backtests/pipeline/results/*.jsonAll agent outputs (350+ files)
backtests/pipeline/*_runner.pyPipeline runners (turbo, mac_research, audit)
backtests/pipeline/*_v2.pyV2 intelligence systems (memory, rulebook, etc)
dashboard.htmlAuto-generated dashboard (DO NOT edit directly)
notes/session_log.mdSession history (updated every session)
archive/Old versions of everything (never deleted, always archived)

8. DASHBOARD (This page)

Generated by backtests/pipeline/generate_dashboard.py. NEVER edit dashboard.html directly -- it gets overwritten.

  • Regenerated: Every turbo cycle (Phase 7), every Mac research cycle
  • NOT regenerated: On git commits (removed from pre-commit hook 2026-03-30)
  • Auto-refresh: Page refreshes every 30s via meta tag
  • Health checks: agent_dashboard_checker runs every cycle, checks for broken panels

To add a panel: Edit generate_dashboard.py, add DATA_ load + const + panel HTML + renderer IIFE.

Last updated: 2026-03-30. This panel is auto-generated from generate_dashboard.py.
